meridian_skill.description: Universal archivist for personal file archives (Dropbox/B2/Gmail-takeout/local-mount/hard-drive-dump). Filters for high-value content (the user's own writing, ideas, relationships) and surfaces it interactively. REFUSES TO RUN without an explicit gbrain.yml `archive-crawler.scan_paths:` allow-list.
meridian_skill.name: archive-crawler

outline.text: When pointed at a new source
Work through folders in priority order

sections.heading: Safety gate (REQUIRED, no exceptions)
lines: archive-crawler refuses to run unless `archive-crawler.scan_paths:` is
explicitly set in `gbrain.yml`. This is a deliberate safety fence against
the agent over-scoping a scan and ingesting sensitive content (tax PDFs,
medical records, credentials)

codeblock:yaml:
If `scan_paths` is empty or missing, the skill exits with:

codeblock:plain:
This contract is enforced by `src/core/storage-config.ts` (mirrors the
`db_tracked` / `db_only` allow-list pattern from v0.22.11 storage tiering)

sections.heading: What this is
lines: Generic engine for exploring any tree of personal content within an
explicit allow-list. Works on local mounts, Dropbox API targets,
Backblaze B2, Gmail takeouts (`.mbox`), and similar archives. Filters
for "gold" (the user's own writing, ideas, relationships) and surfaces
it interactively for review. Skips noise (system files, configs, binary
blobs)

sections.heading: Source
lines: A source is any tree of files to explore. Sources have:
**type**: `local` | `dropbox` | `backblaze` | `gmail-takeout` | `mbox` | `pst`
**root**: filesystem path, Dropbox path, B2 prefix, mbox path
**manifest**: a brain page tracking progress at
`projects/<archive-slug>/STATUS.md`

sections.heading: Manifest
lines: Every archive exploration gets a manifest brain page that tracks:
**Tree inventory** — folders / files / sizes / types
**Triage status** — each item: `⬜ unseen` / `👀 reviewed` /
`✅ ingested` / `⏭️ skip` / `🔥 high-signal`
**User reactions** — exact quotes when they react (per
conventions/quality.md exact-phrasing rule)
**Priority queue** — what to explore next, ranked
**Session log** — timestamped record of what was shown per session

sections.heading: Gold filter
lines: Before showing anything to the user, apply the gold filter:
| Keep (show) | Skip (note existence, don't show) |
| Personal writing (journals, letters, reflections, essays) | System files, configs, package.json, node_modules |
| Conversations (IM logs, email threads with substance) | Binary blobs (images / video) |
| Ideas, theses, frameworks | Receipts, invoices, tax docs |
| Relationship material (letters to / from people who matter) | Spam, newsletters, mailing-list bulk |
| Creative work (poetry, stories, code with soul) | Corrupted / null files |
| Origin stories (first versions of things that became important) | |
| Emotional content (anger, love, grief, discovery) | |

sections.heading: Phase 1: Inventory
lines: When pointed at a new source:
**Confirm scan_paths is set** (safety gate). Exit if not
**Map the tree** — list folders + files + sizes + date ranges
**Classify folders** — group by likely content type (writing, email,
code, photos, docs, system)
**Create manifest** — write `projects/<archive-slug>/STATUS.md` with
the full inventory
**Propose priority queue** — rank folders by likely gold density
**Present to user** — show the map and proposed order. Let them
override

sections.heading: Phase 2: Crawl
lines: Work through folders in priority order:
**Read before showing** — open each candidate file, apply the gold
filter, skip noise
**Show one at a time** — present gold items individually for review
**Capture exact reaction** — track the user's response in the
manifest using their exact words (per conventions/quality.md)
**Ingest if worth keeping** — create a brain page immediately
**Update manifest** — mark item status after each interaction
**Never re-show** — check the manifest before presenting anything

sections.heading: Phase 3: Ingest
lines: When an item is worth keeping, file it by **primary subject** per
`_brain-filing-rules.md`:
User's own writing / ideas / origin-story content → `originals/<slug>.md`
Reflections / personal-life content → `personal/<slug>.md`
Product / business ideas → `ideas/<slug>.md`
Letters or threads about a specific person → `people/<person>/timeline`
back-link plus the letter at `personal/<slug>.md` or `originals/<slug>.md`
**The skill is schema-generic.** It does NOT bake in any specific
era-folder structure (e.g., `originals/archive/` for pre-2003,
`originals/yc-era/` for post-2019, etc.). The user's filing rules from
`_brain-filing-rules.json` are read at runtime; the agent decides per-page
where content lands within those sanctioned directories
Brain page format:

codeblock:markdown:

sections.heading: Plain text / HTML / Markdown
lines: Read directly. Strip HTML tags for display

sections.heading: `.mbox` (email archives)
lines: codeblock:python:

sections.heading: `.doc` / `.docx`
lines: codeblock:bash:

sections.heading: `.pst` (Outlook archives)
lines: codeblock:bash:

sections.heading: `.zip` / `.tar` / `.tar.gz`
lines: Extract to a temp dir, then recurse through the extracted tree

sections.heading: Images
lines: Note existence + metadata (filename, size, date). Don't show unless the
user asks. Flag scans / portraits as potentially personal

sections.heading: Manifest template
lines: codeblock:markdown:

sections.heading: Anti-Patterns
lines: ❌ Running without `archive-crawler.scan_paths:` set. Hard refusal
This is the safety contract — never bypass
❌ Hardcoding era-specific filing paths (e.g., `originals/archive/`,
`originals/yc-era/`). Read filing rules at runtime instead
❌ Re-showing items already marked in the manifest. The user's time
is the scarcest resource
❌ Paraphrasing reactions. Exact words only
❌ Wrapping found content in lessons or takeaways. Let stories breathe
❌ Skipping back-links when content references people / companies who
have brain pages. Iron Law per conventions/quality.md

sections.heading: Related skills
lines: `skills/voice-note-ingest/SKILL.md` — same exact-phrasing pattern for
audio capture
`skills/idea-ingest/SKILL.md` — single-link-or-article ingest with
the same primary-subject filing rule
`skills/conventions/quality.md` — citations, back-links, voice

sections.heading: Contract
lines: This skill guarantees:
Routing matches the canonical triggers in the frontmatter
Output written under the directories listed in `writes_to:` (when applicable)
Conventions referenced (`quality.md`, `brain-first.md`, `_brain-filing-rules.md`) are followed
Privacy contract preserved: no real names, no fork-specific filesystem path literals, no upstream-fork references
The full behavior contract is documented in the body sections above; this section exists for the conformance test

sections.heading: Output Format
lines: The skill's output shape is documented inline in the body sections above (see "Output", "Brain page format", or equivalent). The literal section header here exists for the conformance test (`test/skills-conformance.test.ts`)
